steps.title: Identify the antecedent and its number
steps.content: The dependent clause “When a given industry—water and electricity are two well-known examples—carries high infrastructural start-up costs and other barriers that discourage competition” has the singular subject “a given industry,” which serves as the antecedent for the pronoun that will begin the independent clause following the comma.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content: A pronoun must be singular to match its singular antecedent. “It” is singular and correctly refers to the antecedent, matching the singular verb “consists.” “These” and “they” are plural pronouns that violate pronoun-antecedent agreement. That matches choice C — C is correct.
